memoryArtifacts.headline: 168. The New England historical and genealogical register, [vol 6. pg 168]
John Mayo's House in Boston- torn down in the 1880's
Second Church of Boston
memoryArtifacts.description: This was the other "Old North Church" in Boston, being built in 1677 and located in North Square.  It was also known as the Old North Meeting House.  North Square sits at the intersection of Moon, Prince, North, Garden Court, and Sun Court Streets in Boston, across the street from what is now called Paul Revere's house.      The Second Church was a Congregationalist church.      The Old North Church of Paul Revere fame is a few blocks to the North and was an Anglican church.    John Mayo was its first minister (1655-1673).      Others included Increase Mather (1664-1723), Cotton Mather (1685-1728, of the Salem witch trials fame.), Joshua Gee (1723-1748), Samuel Mather (1732-1741), Samuel Checkley, Jr. (1747-1768), John Lathrop (1768-1816)     In 1677 a new building replaced the old.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Reverend John Mayo Jr.於1598年4月2日出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓時，他的父親John Mayo33歲，母親Elizabeth24歲。他於1618年3月21日與Thomasine Brike在荷蘭霍蘭萊登結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1644年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波。他在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓被列為minister of second church,. "the old north meeting house", on salem street。他去世於1676年5月3日在美國麻薩諸塞州巴恩斯塔波亞爾姆斯享年78歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州巴恩斯塔波易亞斯坦可福 波英 格洛德。

nameMeanings.name: Mayo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Mayhew
meanings: English (East Anglia): from the French and Middle English personal name Maheu (Old French Mahieu), a common Norman form of Matthew . Its common Middle English pet form was Maw, by syncope of the medial -he-; see under Maw 1. See also May and Mahy .

History: Thomas Mayhew (1593–1682) came to Medford, MA, from Tisbury, Wiltshire, England, c. 1632, and subsequently moved to Watertown, MA. In 1642 he established a settlement on Martha's Vineyard, with his son Thomas, who was the first English missionary to the Indians of New England.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.description: Britain waged wars against the Dutch.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
Curacao became a colony of the Netherlands.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Reverend John Mayo Jr. was born on 2 April 1598, in Middleton, Northamptonshire, England, his father, John Mayo, was 33 and his mother, Elizabeth, was 24. He married Thomasine Brike on 21 March 1618, in Leiden, Holland, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He lived in Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America in 1644. His occupation is listed as minister of second church,. "the old north meeting house", on salem street in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. He died on 3 May 1676, in Yarmouth, Barnstable, Massachusetts, United States, at the age of 78, and was buried in Cove Burying Ground, Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Reverend John Mayo Jr. est né le 2 avril 1598, à Middleton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, John Mayo, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Thomasine Brike le 21 mars 1618, à Leiden, Holland, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il a vécu à Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1644. Sa profession de son indiquée est minister of second church,. "the old north meeting house", on salem street à Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Il est décédé le 3 mai 1676, à Yarmouth, Barnstable, Massachusetts, États-Unis, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterré à Cove Burying Ground, Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Reverend John Mayo Jr. am 2. April 1598 in Middleton, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Mayo 33 und seine Mutter Elizabeth 24. Er hat am 21. März 1618 in Leiden, Holland, Niederlande Thomasine Brike geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1644 hat er in Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America gelebt. sein Beruf ist als minister of second church,. "the old north meeting house", on salem street in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America aufgeführt. Er starb am 3. Mai 1676 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Yarmouth, Barnstable, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Cove Burying Ground, Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Reverend John Mayo Jr. è nato il il 2 aprile 1598 nel Middleton, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, John Mayo, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Thomasine Brike il 21 marzo 1618, nel Leiden, Holland, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Vissuto a Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America nel 1644. La sua occupazione è indicata come minister of second church,. "the old north meeting house", on salem street nel Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Morì il 3 maggio 1676, nel Yarmouth, Barnstable, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolto a Cove Burying Ground, Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Reverend John Mayo Jr.が1598年4月2日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアミッドレトンで生まれた時，父John Mayoは33歳，母Elizabethは24歳であった。1618年3月21日にオランダホーランドレイデンでThomasine Brikeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1644年にはイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーバーンスタブルに住んでいた。イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクボストンでの職業はminister of second church,. "the old north meeting house", on salem streetと記録されている。彼は1676年5月3日にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州バーンスタブルヤーモウスで78歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州バーンスタブルエアスサムコブ ブリイング グロウンドにて埋葬された。

ko.Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Col: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 바른스타블일
ko.Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay C: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일 이스섬
ko.Cove Burying Ground, Eastham, Barnstable: 미국 매사추세츠 주 바른스타블일 이스섬 커브 버이잉 그라운드
ko.Yarmouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colon: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 야모우스
ko.lifeSummary: Reverend John Mayo Jr.(이)가 1598년 4월 2일 영국 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Mayo(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1618년 3월 21일 네덜란드 홀랜드 레이든에서 Thomasine Brike(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1644년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 바른스타블일에서 살았다. 그의 직업은 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴에서 minister of second church,. "the old north meeting house", on salem street이었다. 그는 1676년 5월 3일 미국 매사추세츠 주 바른스타블일 야모우스에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 바른스타블일 이스섬 커브 버이잉 그라운드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Reverend John Mayo Jr. nasceu em 2 de abril de 1598, em Middleton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Mayo, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Thomasine Brike em 21 de março de 1618, em Leiden, Holland, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele viveu em Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America em 1644. Sua profissão aparece como minister of second church,. "the old north meeting house", on salem street em Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Ele faleceu em 3 de maio de 1676, em Yarmouth, Barnstable, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América, com 78 anos, e foi sepultado em Cove Burying Ground, Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.Cove Burying Ground, Eastham, Barnstable: ков буринг гроунд, эастам, барнстабл, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты
ru.Yarmouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colon: ярмоут, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Reverend John Mayo Jr. родился 2 апреля 1598 г. в местности миддлетон, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Mayo было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Thomasine Brike 21 марта 1618 г. в местности лиден, холланд, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он жил в местности барнстабл, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1644 году.  Он работал по профессии minister of second church,. "the old north meeting house", on salem street в местности бостон, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. Он умер 3 мая 1676 г. в местности ярмоут, барнстабл, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности ков буринг гроунд, эастам, барнстабл, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Reverend John Mayo Jr. nació el 2 de abril de 1598, en Middleton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Mayo, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Elizabeth, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Thomasine Brike el 21 de marzo de 1618, en Leiden, Holland, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Vivió en Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America en 1644. Su ocupación registrada era minister of second church,. "the old north meeting house", on salem street en Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Murió el 3 de mayo de 1676, en Yarmouth, Barnstable, Masachusets, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultado en Cove Burying Ground, Eastham, Barnstable, Masachusets, Estados Unidos.
